media.base_medium: ASW (artificial seawater) medium for marine Synechococcus WH7803, cited as ref 47 (Wilson, Carr & Mann 1996, J Phycol 32:506-516); recipe lineage from Wyman/Carr; N and P adjusted to 8.8 mM nitrate and 0.18 mM phosphate, no vitamins.

sources_chased.what: base ASW recipe cited as ref 47 (Wilson, Carr & Mann 1996, J Phycol 32:506-516)
sources_chased.how: ref not open-access; identical ASW-for-WH7803 recipe recovered verbatim from PLOS ONE pone.0281537 (Methods) and cross-checked against ASM Spectrum spectrum.00187-22 usage
WebFetch PLOS ONE pone.0281537 Methods (1 mL trace metal solution per L; no vitamins) — consistent with paper's 'ASW contained no vitamin supplements'

missing_before: explicit KCl / MgSO4 / NaNO3 salt forms
media.notes: Full ASW recipe recovered by chasing ref 47 (Wilson/Carr/Mann 1996); base composition confirmed verbatim from an identical WH7803 ASW formulation (PLOS ONE pone.0281537). Base 0.75 g/L NaNO3 = 8.82 mM matches the paper's stated 8.8 mM nitrate exactly, confirming the recipe identity; phosphate set to 0.18 mM per main text. Medium is vitamin-free (stated in paper) and has no organic carbon source: Synechococcus is a photoautotroph fixing atmospheric CO2 under light. Ammonium/HCO3 exchanges in the prior draft are not medium ingredients (ammonium arises only via heterotroph remineralisation in co-culture); N is supplied solely as nitrate.
